title: orchis papilionacea subsp. grandiflora
intro: Orchis papilionacea subsp. grandiflora is a subspecies of the Butterfly Orchid (Orchis papilionacea), a type of terrestrial orchid native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its showy, purple flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Orchis papilionacea subsp. grandiflora include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to topical creams and ointments for external use.
